title: Can EPFO recover PF money after employer’s violation? Telangana HC delivers key ruling
summary: The Telangana High Court has ruled that EPFO cannot recover provident fund dues from an employee for an employer's alleged violation of EPF rules. The court said liability for transferring PF accumulations after surrender of the exemption rests with the employer and trust.

title: RBI flags rising money mule fraud: Why sharing your bank account can be dangerous
summary: Banks and regulators are warning about the risks of money mule accounts in online fraud as digital transactions rise. The RBI advises customers to safeguard their accounts and be cautious of suspicious requests, as fraud cases remain a significant concern in the banking sector.

title: Money-market fund vs short-duration fund: Where to park your cash and why
summary: Money-market funds primarily invest in short-term instruments maturing within one year, while short-duration funds invest in a mix of debt securities and money-market instruments with a portfolio duration of 1 to 3 years.

title: Why FCNR deposits at 6-7.1% rates are attractive for NRIs
summary: For most NRIs, the right way to use this window is to use their disposable savings to make a deposit for three to five years and leave the leverage to those equipped for its risks.

title: Gold curbs won't work. Financial innovation will
summary: India's instinct to curb gold imports via higher duties and moral appeals is self-defeating—it drives up domestic prices, spurs exchange-traded fund inflows, and increases physical bullion demand. The solution is to shift gold ETFs to derivatives-backed structures to mobilise domestically held gold.

title: Real-time lending: Why modern digital infrastructure is the future of credit
summary: The lending industry is shifting to real-time infrastructure to meet borrower demands for speed and data security. APIs and modern platforms enable quick access to financial data, enhancing risk management and customer experiences.

summary: Today marks the final day for stakeholders to submit their memorandum to the 8th Pay Commission. Major proposals include increasing gratuity payouts to ₹75 lakh and a revised calculation formula for government employees. &nbsp;

title: Gold jumps over 2% as US-Iran peace deal boosts bullion demand
summary: Gold and silver prices surged over 2% on MCX after a US-Iran peace framework eased inflation fears and lowered rate-hike expectations. Falling oil prices, softer yields and a weaker dollar supported bullion, while jewellers reported improved customer footfalls and renewed buying interest.
